actions.title: Fix cron PATH in spawn-task.sh
actions.plan: 1. Add PATH export after set -euo pipefail in spawn-task.sh. 2. Verify script still passes bash -n syntax check.
actions.spawn_prompt:
Fix the cron PATH issue in `~/.openclaw/monitor/spawn-task.sh` that causes 'tmux: command not found' when called from cron.

After line 13 (`set -euo pipefail`), add:
```bash
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

This ensures tmux, openclaw, gh, and other Homebrew-installed tools are available when the script runs from a minimal cron PATH.

Acceptance criteria:
1. `bash -n ~/.openclaw/monitor/spawn-task.sh` returns 0 (no syntax errors)
2. `env -i HOME=$HOME PATH=/usr/bin:/bin bash -c 'source ~/.openclaw/monitor/spawn-task.sh 2>&1; echo $PATH' 2>&1 | head -5` shows /opt/homebrew/bin on PATH
3. The PATH export appears before any command that uses tmux, gh, or openclaw
actions.success_metric: No more 'tmux: command not found' errors in monitor.log on next cron cycle

actions.title: Fix active-tasks.json false positive for PR #44
actions.plan: 1. Edit active-tasks.json. 2. Update cf-architecture-latest-5 entry: status='done', pr=44, prUrl=PR URL.
actions.spawn_prompt:
Fix a monitoring false positive in `~/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/theapp/.clawdbot/active-tasks.json`.

Find the task with `"id": "cf-architecture-latest-5"`. It currently has `"status": "failed"` and `"pr": null`, but PR #44 was successfully created at https://github.com/patrickkidd/btcopilot/pull/44.

Update the entry:
- Change `"status": "failed"` to `"status": "done"`
- Change `"pr": null` to `"pr": 44`
- Add `"prUrl": "https://github.com/patrickkidd/btcopilot/pull/44"`

Acceptance criteria:
1. The JSON file is valid (python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$HOME/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/theapp/.clawdbot/active-tasks.json'))" succeeds)
2. The cf-architecture-latest-5 task shows status 'done' with pr: 44
actions.success_metric: Task registry accurately reflects PR #44 creation

actions.title: Clean up 3 stale worktrees
actions.plan: 1. git worktree remove for T999, cf-architecture-latest-3, and cf-architecture-latest-5. 2. Verify with git worktree list.
actions.spawn_prompt:
Clean up 3 orphaned git worktrees in the btcopilot repo. All associated tmux sessions are dead and no agents are running.

From `/Users/hurin/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/theapp/btcopilot`, run:
```bash
git worktree remove /Users/hurin/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/btcopilot-worktrees/T999 --force
git worktree remove /Users/hurin/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/btcopilot-worktrees/cf-architecture-latest-3 --force
git worktree remove /Users/hurin/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/btcopilot-worktrees/cf-architecture-latest-5 --force
```

Then verify: `git worktree list` should show only the main working tree.

Also delete the corresponding remote branches that are no longer needed:
```bash
git push origin --delete feat/T999 2>/dev/null || true
git push origin --delete feat/cf-architecture-latest-3 2>/dev/null || true
```
Do NOT delete `feat/cf-architecture-latest-5` — PR #44 is still open on that branch.

Acceptance criteria:
1. `git worktree list` shows only the main worktree
2. `ls ~/.openclaw/workspace-hurin/btcopilot-worktrees/` shows no remaining directories (or only cf-architecture-latest-5 if git worktree remove fails for it due to open PR)
3. No errors in the removal process
actions.success_metric: Stale worktrees removed, disk space reclaimed

actions.title: Remove unused conversation_id param from Engine.ask()
actions.plan: 1. Read engine.py to confirm conversation_id is unused after PR #44's dead code removal. 2. Remove the parameter from ask() signature. 3. Find and update all callers. 4. Run engine tests.
actions.spawn_prompt:
Address the Gemini code review feedback on PR #44 (https://github.com/patrickkidd/btcopilot/pull/44). After the dead code removal in that PR, the `conversation_id` parameter in `Engine.ask()` is no longer used.

Work on the `feat/cf-architecture-latest-5` branch (which is PR #44's branch).

1. Read `btcopilot/pro/copilot/engine.py` and find the `ask()` method signature
2. Confirm `conversation_id` is not referenced in the method body
3. Remove `conversation_id` from the `ask()` method signature
4. Search the entire codebase for callers of `Engine.ask()` or `.ask(` that pass `conversation_id` — update them to remove the argument
5. Run tests: `uv run pytest btcopilot/tests/pro/copilot/test_engine.py -x -q`
6. Commit and push to the existing PR branch

Acceptance criteria:
1. `conversation_id` no longer appears in `Engine.ask()` signature
2. All callers updated (grep for `conversation_id` in pro/copilot/ returns no hits)
3. `uv run pytest btcopilot/tests/pro/copilot/test_engine.py -x -q` passes
4. Changes pushed to `feat/cf-architecture-latest-5` branch (PR #44)
actions.success_metric: PR #44 addresses Gemini review feedback and is ready for clean merge

actions.title: Land T7-12 backend tests on btcopilot master
actions.plan: 1. Cherry-pick commit 0fa8ac1 (T7-12 test file) from feature/t7-12-auto-detect-clusters to a new branch. 2. Verify tests pass. 3. Create PR against master.
actions.spawn_prompt:
The T7-12 feature (auto-detect event clusters on PDP accept) was merged on the familydiagram side (PR #86) but the backend test file never made it to btcopilot master — PR #32 was closed.

The test file is at commit `0fa8ac1` on branch `feature/t7-12-auto-detect-clusters` in the btcopilot repo at `/Users/hurin/Projects/theapp/btcopilot`.

1. Create a new branch from master: `git checkout -b feat/t7-12-backend-tests master`
2. Cherry-pick the test commit: `git cherry-pick 0fa8ac1`
3. If there are merge conflicts, resolve them (the test file `btcopilot/tests/personal/test_clusters.py` should apply cleanly since it's a new file)
4. Run the tests: `uv run pytest btcopilot/tests/personal/test_clusters.py -x -q`
5. If tests fail due to missing backend code, also cherry-pick other necessary commits from the T7-12 branch
6. Push and create a PR: `gh pr create --title 'T7-12: Add auto-detect cluster backend tests' --body '## Summary\n- Cherry-pick T7-12 test coverage from closed PR #32\n- Tests for auto-detect event clusters on PDP accept\n\n## Test plan\n- [x] uv run pytest btcopilot/tests/personal/test_clusters.py -x -q\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)'`

Acceptance criteria:
1. `test_clusters.py` exists on master (or a PR branch targeting master)
2. Tests pass locally
3. PR created and CI triggered
actions.success_metric: T7-12 backend test coverage lands on btcopilot master, completing the feature across both repos

actions.title: Fix failure capture to read task logs instead of dead tmux
actions.plan: 1. Edit capture_tmux_output() in check-agents.py. 2. When tmux capture-pane fails, fall back to reading last 100 lines of task-logs/{task_id}.log. 3. This gives the Ralph Loop actual diagnostic data.
actions.spawn_prompt:
Fix the failure capture in `~/.openclaw/monitor/check-agents.py` so the Ralph Loop gets real diagnostic data instead of 'Session was already dead' placeholders.

The current `capture_tmux_output()` function writes useless placeholders like `[Session 'claude-cf-architecture-latest-5' was already dead when capture attempted]` when a tmux session has already exited. But the task's actual output is available in `~/.openclaw/monitor/task-logs/{task_id}.log`.

Edit the `capture_tmux_output()` function. After the existing tmux capture attempt fails (the else/failure branch), instead of writing a useless placeholder, try to read the task log file:

```python
def capture_tmux_output(session, task_id):
    """Capture last 100 lines from tmux session or task log. Save to failures dir."""
    FAILURES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    failure_log = FAILURES_DIR / f"{task_id}.log"

    # Try live tmux capture first
    code, output, _ = run(f"tmux capture-pane -t '{session}' -p -S -100")
    if code == 0 and output:
        failure_log.write_text(output)
        log(f"  Captured tmux output to {failure_log}")
        return str(failure_log)

    # Session already dead — fall back to task log file
    task_log = Path.home() / f".openclaw/monitor/task-logs/{task_id}.log"
    if task_log.exists():
        lines = task_log.read_text().splitlines()
        last_100 = '\n'.join(lines[-100:])
        failure_log.write_text(f"[From task log - session was dead]\n{last_100}")
        log(f"  Captured from task log to {failure_log}")
        return str(failure_log)

    failure_log.write_text(f"[Session '{session}' dead and no task log found]\n")
    log(f"  No capture source available for {failure_log}")
    return str(failure_log)
```

Make sure `Path` is already imported (it should be — check the imports at the top). Preserve the existing function signature and return type.

Acceptance criteria:
1. `python3 -c "import check_agents; print('import OK')"` succeeds when run from `~/.openclaw/monitor/`
2. The function falls back to task-logs when tmux session is dead
3. No other functions are modified
actions.success_metric: Future failure logs contain actual CC output, enabling real Ralph Loop diagnosis
